programme.desc: Dick and Arthur race to collect low-hanging fruit to make redcurrant jelly, while in south-west France, Donna and Paul are hard at work creating a tea room.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: The Strawbridge family heads to the beaches of Saint-Malo with Angel's parents to seek out chateau inspiration. Ithaca and Alex's fundraiser in northern France is a logistical nightmare, but becomes a hot ticket. First impressions are important to Angela and Steve, who get stuck in a last-minute fix as they try to restore their chateau's electric gates before the first rental guests of the season arrive.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge visits Philip and Angelina and helps them build a bridge at their chateau - will Dick's design work? Jonathan and Michael test the services they provide at their chateau by inviting Michael's old college mates for the weekend, while Jonny and Ashley continue the search for their dream chateau.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Arvada, Colorado, where a husband and wife have allowed their restaurant to fall into disrepair. Randy's hoarding is out of control, while Alexa, who runs the kitchen, has a lot to learn. To make matters worse, the Old Neighborhood is one of the filthiest eateries the fiery chef has ever seen, and only one of the biggest transformations of the series can save the business.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Queens, New York, where he meets a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband-and-wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, while their daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle and at her wits' end.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gordon Ramsay comes to the aid of Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Woodland Park, Colorado, where guests have a choice between casual fine-dining service or a pick-up from the drive-through takeaway. Hard-nosed owner Julie finds herself completely at odds with the head chef, forcing her to breaking point.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his two-part challenge at Mangia Mangia in Woodland Park, Colorado. In order to save the struggling Italian restaurant from disaster, he must persuade harassed owner Julie to co-operate with the head chef, and work his magic on a menu that relies heavily on a microwave.(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, with cameras capturing their instant reactions to Killing Eve, Too Hot to Handle off Netflix, The Yorkshire Vet, The Andrew Marr Show with Michael Gove, Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins, and BBC News on face masks.(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Comedian James Acaster, actor Russell Tovey, TV presenter Rylan Clark-Neal and actress Michelle Keegan take on three challenges as the contest continues. They face three tough tasks set by judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood - topped flapjacks for the signature challenge and a tricky technical. They are then asked to create their 'happy places' out of meringue for the showstopper challenge. Who will flap at the first hurdle, and who will battle their way through to be crowned star baker? Presented by Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding.(n)

programme.title: Joe Lycett's Got Your Back
programme.desc: Style guru Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen helps Joe as he delivers his own brand of justice to a parcel delivery company. One-time Treasure Hunt star Anneka Rice dons a jumpsuit once more as Joe takes her on a hunt for the UK's disappearing cash machines. Plus, comedian Rosie Jones gets into a lather over unethical beauty products.(n)

programme.title: Can Science Beat the Virus?
programme.desc: A special debate about the coronavirus pandemic, with only scientists on the panel. As Britain increasingly looks beyond politics to science for reassurance about how and when the crisis will end, this programme brings together experts to take part in a straight-talking, fact-based discussion providing scientific answers to the most difficult questions. The discussion covers the psychology of isolation, the technical issues behind mass production of ventilators, and, crucially, gives viewers the chance to explore the fascinating science behind this extraordinary outbreak.(n)

programme.title: Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: Twelve more famous faces are put through special forces training at a derelict farmstead in the Inner Hebrides under chief instructor Ant Middleton and his team of Foxy, Billy, Ollie and Jay. In the first edition, Tony Bellew, Helen Skelton, Joey Essex, Yasmin Evans, John Fashanu, Katie Price, Jack Maynard, Nikki Sanderson, Brendan Cole, Lauren Steadman, Leon 'Locksmith' Rolle and Anthea Turner face a first challenge of a 50-metre cliff free-fall.(n)

programme.title: Shut-Ins: Britain's Fattest People
programme.desc: The documentary focuses on Andrew, who weighs 36 stone and feels like his life has come to a halt because of his obesity. A surgeon recommends a new and experimental surgery to help him regain his life, but for the operation to go ahead, Andrew has to prove he is committed to sticking to a high-protein diet for the rest of his life.(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton heads to the world's biggest bread factory, finding out why some loaves fall to pieces at first contact with a knife, while others can happily survive a good spreading. Jimmy Doherty is in Turkey, investigating the intoxicating properties of the poppy seeds in bagels, while Matt Tebbutt sets out to find the secrets behind the writing on some cakes - is it the work of man or machine?(n)

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Amateurs compete with chef Clodagh McKenna to create different versions of prawn, chorizo and halloumi wraps, before the Food Jury blind taste both dishes.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Ben and Ambika kick off the competition in Hertfordshire with a south-east Asian menu. Their guests are Sinead from Northern Ireland and her prank-loving partner Tony, and competitive couple Kally and Mark. The lads bond over football, and by the end of the night Tony has played a prank or two setting the tone for the week. Subsequently, Kally and Mark put on an Anglo-Indian menu, but things soon get heated in the kitchen. Then, Tony and Sinead serve up an Irish menu, with silly dances, silly jokes and silly accents. However, it remains to be seen which couple will pocket the prize for best soiree.(n)

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In north Devon, married couple Frankie and Jason kick off the competition. Outspoken duo Kate and Ian don't let the evening's many delays go unnoticed, and as tensions rise around the table, internet lovebirds Chris and Amy try to stay firmly out of the way. Day two sees Kate and Ian serve up a menu full of rustic French cuisine, influenced by their 22 years living in France. But a table laying mishap suggests that Kate has overtly snubbed her guests, although tensions are eased when former falconers Kate and Ian bring out one of their favourite birds of prey. Finally it's dog lovers Chris and Amy's turn to step up to the plate, and believing themselves to be the foodies of the group they're confident they can bag the prize for best soiree.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Marianne and Anthony from West Yorkshire search for their perfect holiday home in the Axarquia region of southern Spain.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies draws on her property expertise to help South Wales couple Neil and Julie find a holiday home around Almunecar on southern Spain's Costa Tropical.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton help a couple to choose between a property on the western coastline of Wales and a home in Carcassonne in the south of France.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Tracey and Stephen from Staffordshire search for a new home in the Spanish province of Malaga with a budget of Â£100,000.(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Redondo Beach, California, for the first of two visits to Zayna Flaming Grill, a Mediterranean restaurant owned and run by aunt and niece Faye and Brenda. Faye believes she has the weight of the entire restaurant on her shoulders, while Brenda feels she isn't treated as an equal in the business. With tensions rising, the food not up to scratch and everyone playing the blame game, the chef is faced with one of the trickiest situations he's ever encountered.(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay concludes his visit to Zayna Flaming Grill in Redondo Beach, California, and finds more questions than answers as he tries to save the crisis-ridden restaurant from disaster. programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

programme.title: When Will Lockdown End?
programme.desc: Dr Xand van Tulleken explores why the coronavirus lockdown is continuing, as he seeks answers to the questions many people have about when lockdown will end and what the world will look like by then. Will people who have had the virus be allowed more freedom of movement than those who haven't? What kind of restrictions will we have on work and movement, and for how long? Xand looks at both sides of the debate, meeting scientists, experts and members of the government; and also shares helpful advice on how to cope best with the challenges of the situation we face.(n)

programme.title: The Truth About Amazon
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant and Helen Skelton present a guide to the online retailer, showing how to track prices and investigating how much the company can be trusted. programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.desc: Gareth and Mitch are on the trail of Â£2,500 in unpaid parking tickets in Cardiff, and while the debtor is not at home, they notice his car is sporting false number plates and call the police. The duo also face an angry defendant in Swindon when trying to collect Â£2,000 for unpaid rent on a lock-up, while in Buckinghamshire, Matt and Garry consider seizing a haul of luxury watches to pay more than Â£16,000 owed to a landlord.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor visit Nottingham to chase almost Â£8,000 owed by a car dealership to a dissatisfied customer, while on the Isle of Wight, Brian and Del try to collect Â£3,700 of unpaid estate agency fees. The job begins peacefully, until one of the agents is physically assaulted by the debtor's wife. In Lancashire, Stewart and Iain struggle to identify the person who owes almost Â£2,000 after a failed compensation claim, while Del and Dael try to recover nearly Â£3,500 from a businessman in West London.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Elmor attempt to collect Â£5,000 owed by a debtor in north Wales, but the situation spirals out of control when a group of friends try to take on the agents. Meanwhile, Del and Dael head to south-east London to chase nearly Â£7,000 owed by a man to his ex-girlfriend, before visiting a woman who owes her solicitor almost Â£4,000 after a compensation claim went wrong. In Manchester, Stewart and Vic face a hostile reception at a family run garage that owes Â£6,000 in professional fees.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In south London, Del and Dael Anglin receive a hostile reception from the brother of a debtor who has alreasy defaulted on a promise to pay Â£2,000 she owes to a storage company. In Worcestershire, Gareth and Craig try to recover Â£45,000 from a young online entrepreneur, who claims no responsibility for the debt he owes to his courier. Their target cooks up a flimsy lie in an effort to absolve himself, but dishonesty proves to be the least of the agents' worries when they discover a stash of guns at the back of the garage. In east London, an Indian restaurant owner begins to take his financial responsibilities seriously when Del and Dael start removing cookers and freezers from his premises, and a pair of pensioners in Oxfordshire hope the High Court will provide them a lifeline as they try to pay off a Â£4,000 bill to a solicitors' firm after a failed compensation claim.(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Gareth and Craig investigate a possible case of mistaken identity in Shropshire, then in south Wales find themselves embroiled in a bitter dispute between two dog owners over a vet's bill. Steve and Phil have to evict a distraught young family from a former high street bank in west London, before paying a call on an elderly tailor in north London.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: A furious landlord discovers his tenant has been subletting his exclusive north-west London flat - having not paid any rent for months. In Woolwich, squatters with a baby are given two weeks to find somewhere new to live, while a High Court enforcement officer helps an Essex grandmother evict her non-paying tenants in Dagenham.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Documentary about the horrors of the rental market including a landlord who decided to sell his east London flat so he could afford a property with his girlfriend. But when he told his tenant, things quickly turned sour and the man stopped paying rent and refused to leave. Plus, the story of a pub owner in Lincolnshire trying to evict his tenant, having rented it 10 years ago on a 'gentleman's handshake'. Now, however, he is owed a staggering Â£40,000 in unpaid rent.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Brent Council officers find a severely overcrowded house in north London, while Harrow Council try to help a family forced to sleep in a single room due to leaks coming from the roof. Their landlord is reluctant to complete the work and has instead served them with an eviction notice. Plus, the case of a Birmingham landlord who wants rid of his troublesome tenants, who have not paid rent for more than a year.(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In north London, Harrow council officers investigate an overcrowded property. An inspection uncovers many issues, including an unauthorised loft conversion that is a danger to the tenants who sleep there. A couple in Suffolk agree to rent a property to its former owner, who now refuses to leave, and a single mum tricks her way into a Milton Keynes property, confirming she has paid the deposit having done no such thing.(n)

programme.desc: A psychotherapist is shot dead, and his wife stands trial for murder when it is revealed the man was involved in an inappropriate relationship with one of his teenage clients. programme.desc: Police action to close down an illegal dog-fighting ring leads to a murder inquiry when officers find a woman's finger inside one of the creatures. programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the stabbing of a college activist, whose knowledge of a beef supplier's poor sanitary conditions may have led to his death. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An investigation into the rape and murder of a woman revives harrowing memories for Ducky, who once worked on a similar case in which the killer was never captured and vowed to return. Bafflingly, both victims were civilians dressed in the uniform of a lieutenant, and each had a trident symbol carved into their neck. Starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum.(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An accident at a Navy bombing range brings to light an area used by the Mafia to dump bodies. One of the corpses is identified as a missing FBI agent, so the team investigates the events leading up to his death. Crime drama, guest starring Joe Spano, with Mark Harmon.(n)

programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a struggling actress in what appears to have been a bungled robbery. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's wife kills an intruder and later claims he tried to rape her. The team embarks on a mystifying wild goose chase through a stack of old case files and eventually discovers the woman was leading a double life while her husband was serving overseas.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a pregnant woman, with Benson convinced she was having an affair. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: The team searches for a Jack the Ripper-type killer. programme.title: Columbo: A Matter of Honour (1976)
programme.desc: The sleuth's holiday in Mexico is disrupted by a bizarre death involving a suave retired bullfighter, who has killed his best friend using one of the animals as a murder weapon - but is determined his spotless image and reputation will remain untarnished. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ricardo Montalban, A Martinez and Pedro Armendariz Jr.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Requiem for a Falling Star (1973)
programme.desc: A vicious gossip columnist digs the dirt on an ageing film actress, who responds by taking on her most challenging role yet - as a real-life murderer. But when the actress kills her own assistant in error, she looks for a way to shift the blame. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Anne Baxter, Mel Ferrer, Kevin McCarthy and multi-Oscar-winning costume designer Edith Head in a guest appearance as herself.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Blueprint for Murder (1972)
programme.desc: An architect kills his business partner, and comes up with an elaborate scheme to make Columbo first suspect him, then reject him as the murderer. The villain begins by teasing the detective with hints on where he has hidden the body - hoping that when Columbo fails to find a corpse, he will look elsewhere for a killer. Crime drama, directed by and starring Peter Falk. With Patrick O'Neal and Janis Paige.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: Old Fashioned Murder (1976)
programme.desc: A woman who has devoted her life to running her family's museum is outraged when her brother decides to sell it. Knowing of someone with criminal connections, she arranges to have her brother killed. However, just when she starts to think she has got away with it, a certain shabby sleuth is assigned to the case. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Joyce Van Patten, Celeste Holm and Tim O'Connor.(n)

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan begins to investigate the murder of a police recruit, but the death of a casual girlfriend leaves him too distracted to concentrate, and he bows out of the case. In his absence, Eames steps in to assist Wheeler - and together they follow the trail of evidence to a surprising suspect. Drama, starring Chris Noth, Julianne Nicholson and Kathryn Erbe.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Stabler are faced with the gruesome task of identifying an animal rights activist found decapitated in a park during a romantic rendezvous. The team try to find two potential killers who were in the vicinity - but are later stunned to discover the crime scene was staged to mislead investigators. Drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Mariska Hargitay.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and Fin investigate the case of a woman found raped and murdered underneath a flyover - and discover $1million of a new cancer drug in the boot of her car. Analysis reveals the dead woman's supplies were fake, linking her to a pharmaceutical racket - but another assault gives the detectives a more immediate concern. Hard-hitting drama, starring Christopher Meloni and Ice-T.(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man accused of the sexual abuse of his daughter is subsequently murdered, but laying the blame at the door of the chief suspects proves difficult. Several family members are in the frame, but despite evidence of a gun outside the victim's office, they appear to be innocent.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Compilation programme centred on a day in Nick Knowles's life, in which the team revisits two of the greatest projects from the series, before finding out how the families involved have fared. programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Loughborough to meet a family of five, whose identical twin babies have been diagnosed as having a form of dwarfism, while at the age of 41, dad Mark is recovering from aggressive bowel and liver cancer. Now they are struggling for space. Having heard of their plight, the DIY SOS team turns up to transform their home, backed up by an army of local tradespeople, with designer Gabrielle Blackman on hand to ensure they don't stray too far from her vision.(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles travels to Epsom to meet paramedics Sam and Ben Laws, who have two children, one with special needs. The couple already find it hard, working opposite shifts which mean they often don't see each other for days on end - and to make matters worse, they have found their house to be inadequate for their requirements. After a close friend nominates them for the DIY SOS treatment, Nick and the team - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - turn up to transform it into a home they can enjoy as a family, now and in the future.(n)

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong.(n)

programme.desc: With thousands of UK citizens currently held in jails around the world, this documentary focuses on the moving cases of British prisoners. Those who break the law overseas face potentially lethal consequences - nightmare 'living hell' conditions, disease, abuse, terror, and sometimes even death. programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Punk singer Chris Sweeney takes up the conductor's baton as he prepares to pass himself off as the leader of a classical orchestra, but with no ability to read music or play an instrument, he faces an uphill struggle to master the necessary skills in only four weeks.(n)

programme.title: Property Ladder
programme.desc: Sarah Beeny visits Patrick and Vanessa from Aberdare in South Wales, who have re-mortgaged their home and bought two dilapidated cottages they have not even seen for Â£25,000.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: With electronic cigarettes increasing in popularity, presenter Michael Mosley considers whether they are safe to use. The benefits of caffeine in helping people stay alert are also explored, as well as alternatives to the chemical. Chris van Tulleken investigates whether household chores can legitimately count as exercise, while Saleyha Ahsan offers first aid tips to treat someone suffering from hypothermia. Plus, Gabriel Weston witnesses life-saving surgery that involves removing all the blood from a patient's body.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams looks at the role - and the unusual odd jobs - of country policemen in the South and South East during the Second World War, when rumours of chicken stealing and illegal pig roasts were fairly common diversions from the privations of rationing.(n)

programme.title: Underworld at War
programme.desc: Peter Williams recounts the extraordinary tale of a Chicago-style turf-war gunfight on the Hampshire-Wiltshire border during the Second World War and Ennis Smith reveals how she broke the rules to marry her soldier boyfriend Clarence.(n)

programme.title: Fox
programme.sub-title: Just Another Villain in a Cheap Suit
programme.desc: Drama following the lives of a London gangster and his family. Starring Peter Vaughan, Ray Winstone and Bernard Hill. Originally broadcast in 1980.(n)

programme.title: Strangerland (2015)
programme.desc: A couple move to a remote town in the Australian Outback, but their new life takes a tragic turn when their teenage children disappear during a dust storm. As the townsfolk mount a search, tension and suspicion rise and public opinion quickly turns against the parents. Thriller, starring Nicole Kidman, Joseph Fiennes and Hugo Weaving.(n)

programme.title: Brothers' Day (2015)
programme.desc: A Manchester teenager feels abandoned by his older brother, who has turned to a life of crime. However, when his latest deal ends badly, the younger sibling finds his eyes opened to the impossible choices his brother faces every day. Crime drama, starring Tom Collins and Lewis Fletcher.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: With electronic cigarettes increasing in popularity, presenter Michael Mosley considers whether they are safe to use. The benefits of caffeine in helping people stay alert are also explored, as well as alternatives to the chemical. Chris van Tulleken investigates whether household chores can legitimately count as exercise, while Saleyha Ahsan offers first aid tips to treat someone suffering from hypothermia. Plus, Gabriel Weston witnesses life-saving surgery that involves removing all the blood from a patient's body.(n)

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Exploring the districts of Woolwich, Plumstead and Deptford.(n)

programme.title: News Rewind
programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days.(n)

programme.title: Talking Points
programme.desc: A chance to catch up on debates about the issues behind the headlines.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice.(n)

programme.title: Operation Amsterdam (1958)
programme.desc: A stiff-upper-lipped British Army officer engages in a spot of larceny to aid the war effort. Ordered to Amsterdam, he joins forces with two cunning Dutchmen and steals a secret cache of diamonds from a bank vault, thus preventing the gems falling into Nazi hands. Second World War adventure, starring Peter Finch, Tony Britton and Eva Bartok.(n)

programme.title: Girl in the Headlines (1963)
programme.desc: An inspector investigating the murder of a fashion model is led straight into the lair of an international drug-smuggling ring. Crime drama, starring Ian Hendry, Ronald Fraser, Jeremy Brett, Margaret Johnston and Natasha Perry.(n)

programme.title: A Handful of Dust (1989)
programme.desc: A couple's life is disrupted by a penniless woman who arrives for the weekend, setting in motion a disastrous sequence of events. Drama, starring Judi Dench, whose performance won her a Bafta award, and James Wilby.(n)

programme.title: Time Without Pity (1957)
programme.desc: An alcoholic writer returns home after years living abroad when he learns his estranged son has been convicted of murder and sentenced to hang. Although his attempts at reconciliation are rejected, he spends the last 24 hours before the execution trying to prove his son is innocent by identifying the real killer. Mystery, starring Michael Redgrave, Ann Todd, Leo McKern and Peter Cushing.(n)

programme.title: I Am Pilate (2020)
programme.desc: Short film about a journalist who is sent to the home of former district governor, only for his routine obituary fact check to reveal an extraordinary secret.(n)

programme.title: Faking It
programme.desc: Individuals take up the challenge to reinvent themselves completely.(n)

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series presented by Andrew White, featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain.(n)

programme.title: Doodlebug Summer
programme.desc: Hitler's V1 flying bombs were responsible for the deaths of nearly 6,000 people in London and the South of England. This episode features accounts from the men and women whose job was to fight the V1s from the ground and a report on the impact of a bomb that hit a chapel one Sunday morning, killing 121 people.(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Medical journalist Michael Mosley provides tips on curing cramp, while Dr Saleyha Ahsan shows how to spot the signs of a deep vein thrombosis, and Dr Chris van Tulleken reveals the truth about what is actually contained in herbal supplements sold in the UK. Surgeon Gabriel Weston travel to the US to witness the remarkable treatment for a hospital superbug that cures within hours.(n)

programme.title: Opening Up the Soane
programme.desc: Documentary about the extensive refurbishment of Sir John Soane's Museum to restore the building to its original glory.(n)

programme.title: Dressed as a Girl
programme.desc: Documentary exploring London's drag community, filmed over the course of five years and documenting the personal struggles of the performers.(n)

programme.title: London Real
programme.desc: Broadcaster and entrepreneur Brian Rose is in debate with some of the world's most interesting minds. Tonight, he talks to Eddie Hall.(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman.(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught.(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Comedian Russell Kane and former EastEnders star Rita Simons (Roxy Mitchell) take part in the game show, teaming up with their relatives to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, in which they can win up to Â£30,000 for their chosen charities. Vernon Kay hosts.(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites another line-up of contestants to answer questions that could change their lives - with lifelines Ask the Audience, Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck.(n)
